response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with an impressive open-source track record (founder of GISCollective, D-language libraries, FOSS4G speaker) and deep Linux/full-stack experience, which aligns well with the technical and open-source dimensions of the role. However, this is an executive-level Head of Development position requiring minimum C1 German — the resume states only basic German — and there is no evidence of formal team leadership, people management, or client-facing architecture consulting. UML, requirements management, and IT security/compliance experience are also absent. The language gap alone is disqualifying for a German consulting leadership role.
strengths: Extensive, broad software engineering experience (web, mobile, embedded, high-throughput backend) since 2006
Strong open-source commitment: founded GISCollective, maintains D-language libraries, conference talks and workshops
Solid Linux, DevOps, and CI/CD background with software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development)
concerns: German listed as 'Basic', far below the required C1 minimum for a client-facing German consulting role
No formal team leadership, people management, or executive experience; career is entirely individual-contributor roles
No demonstrated UML, requirements management, or IT security/risk/compliance expertise required by the role
